react.answer:
## Building a Kanban Board (drag & drop) in React **Mental model:** Cards live in a flat list, each tagged with a `col`. Columns are derived by filtering. Drag-and-drop uses the native HTML5 API: `dragstart` stashes the card id in `dataTransfer`; a column's `dragover` (with `preventDefault`) marks it a drop target; `drop` reassigns that card's `col`. No library needed. ### Step 1 — Flat cards + derived columns ```jsx const [cards, setCards] = useState(initial); const inCol = (col) => cards.filter((c) => c.col === col); ``` ### Step 2 — Start the drag ```jsx const onDragStart = (e, id) => e.dataTransfer.setData('text/plain', id); ``` ### Step 3 — Allow drop + handle it `dragover` must `preventDefault` or `drop` never fires. ```jsx const onDrop = (e, col) => { const id = e.dataTransfer.getData('text/plain'); setCards((cs) => cs.map((c) => (c.id === id ? { ...c, col } : c))); };
e.preventDefault()} onDrop={(e) => onDrop(e, col)} /> ``` ### Step 4 — Draggable cards ```jsx
onDragStart(e, card.id)}>{card.text}
``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Drop on same column | Map is a no-op (same `col`). | | Missing preventDefault | Without it on `dragover`, drop won't fire. | | Ordering within column | Track an index/order field for intra-column sorting. | **Interview tip:** The must-know gotcha is calling `e.preventDefault()` in `onDragOver` — it's what makes an element a valid drop target; forgetting it is why "my drop handler never runs."

vue.answer:
## Building a Kanban Board (drag & drop) in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A flat list of cards, each tagged with a `col`; columns are derived by filtering. Native HTML5 DnD: `@dragstart` stashes the id, `@dragover.prevent` marks a drop zone, `@drop` reassigns the card's `col`. ### Step 1 — Cards + filter helper ```js const cards = ref(initial); const inCol = (col) => cards.value.filter((c) => c.col === col); ``` ### Step 2 — Drag start ```vue
{{ card.text }}
``` ### Step 3 — Allow + handle drop `@dragover.prevent` is the Vue shortcut for `preventDefault` (required so drop fires). ```vue
…
``` ### Step 4 — Reassign column ```js function onDrop(e, col) { const id = e.dataTransfer.getData('text/plain'); const card = cards.value.find((c) => c.id === id); if (card) card.col = col; } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Drop same column | No visible change. | | Missing prevent | `@dragover.prevent` is mandatory. | | Ordering | Add an order field for intra-column sort. | **Interview tip:** `@dragover.prevent` is the Vue-idiomatic fix for the "drop never fires" gotcha — the `.prevent` modifier calls `preventDefault`, which is what makes the element a valid drop target.

angular.answer:
## Building a Kanban Board (drag & drop) in Angular **Mental model:** A flat list of cards tagged with `col`; columns derived by filtering. Native HTML5 DnD: `(dragstart)` stashes the id, `(dragover)` with `preventDefault` marks a drop zone, `(drop)` reassigns `col`. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them), no CDK. ### Step 1 — Cards + filter ```typescript cards: Card[] = [ /* ... */ ]; inCol(col: string): Card[] { return this.cards.filter((c) => c.col === col); } ``` ### Step 2 — Drag start ```html
{{ card.text }}
``` ### Step 3 — Allow + handle drop ```html
…
``` ### Step 4 — Reassign ```typescript onDrop(e: DragEvent, col: string): void { const id = e.dataTransfer!.getData('text/plain'); const card = this.cards.find((c) => c.id === id); if (card) { card.col = col; } } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Drop same column | No-op. | | Missing preventDefault | `(dragover)="$event.preventDefault()"` is required. | | Ordering | Add an order field for intra-column sort. | **Interview tip:** Mention Angular CDK's `DragDropModule` (`cdkDropList`/`cdkDrag`) as the production path — but doing it with the raw HTML5 DnD API shows you understand the `dragover` + `preventDefault` mechanics underneath.
